summary: EXP-227 also resolves ISS-1416 (major: 'reduced cavity-Q' mechanism written backwards). Patch B in EXP-227 replaces the V51.1 Update paragraph and removes the sentence asserting that D-region ionization enhancement reduces cavity-Q — that direction contradicts standard Earth–ionosphere waveguide physics, where lowered D-region reflection height plus increased lower-boundary conductivity raises Q (lower attenuation, frequency upshift). The patch deletes the wrong-direction claim entirely rather than re-derive a contested mechanism, leaving the empirical observation (amplitude decrease across multiple stations during the G3+ window) standalone and forcing the WIN to rest on observation rather than on backwards theory. See EXP-227 for full text and the ISS-1415 proposal for the parallel citation-direction fix. The two issues share one findings file because both originate from the same paragraphs and are co-fixed by the same two anchors — closing one without the other would re-introduce the inconsistency.
